title: Card layout component for React dashboards
description: Card component for React and Next.js dashboards: structured headers, media slots, and Tailwind-friendly spacing for summaries and listings.

og.title: Card · Zentauri UI
og.description: Card component for React and Next.js dashboards: structured headers, media slots, and Tailwind-friendly spacing for summaries and listings.

twitter.title: Card · Zentauri UI
twitter.description: Card component for React and Next.js dashboards: structured headers, media slots, and Tailwind-friendly spacing for summaries and listings.

intro: Cards bundle headers, body content, and actions into a predictable surface for dashboards and marketing layouts. Use them in React apps and Next.js pages that need modular content blocks.
useCases: Surface KPI summaries on analytics landing pages.
Group related settings with a clear title and footer actions.
Present catalog items with media, price, and CTA rows.
Stack quick links in responsive grids.

faqs.question: Does the Card component work with Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Yes. Import it like any other React component; keep interactive subtrees in client components when you need hooks or browser APIs, and leave static structure in server components where possible.

faqs.question: Can I customize card with Tailwind CSS?
faqs.answer: Zentauri UI exposes class-friendly variants and slots so you can extend styles with Tailwind utilities without fighting inline styles.

faqs.question: Is this Card implementation accessible by default?
faqs.answer: Primitives follow sensible defaults, but accessibility depends on how you label controls, manage focus, and wire keyboard handlers in your app. Validate critical flows with keyboard-only use and screen readers.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body:
Cards are intentionally dumb containers: push business logic up a layer.

Use consistent padding tokens so grids align across breakpoints.

sections.heading: Composition and API
sections.body:
Prefer compound subcomponents instead of one oversized prop bag. Export a small, documented API for your design system.

Keep heading levels coordinated with surrounding page structure for SEO and accessibility.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.body:
Keyboard order, focus rings, and ARIA attributes should match production usage. Test with your supported browsers and assistive technologies when semantics are non-trivial.

This preview page exposes a single h1 in the hero for a clean outline.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Colocate examples under the App Router, keep server and client boundaries explicit, and avoid pulling interactive overlays into unexpected server layouts.

Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL so canonical and Open Graph URLs resolve on deploy.
